confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_0d5125ed-4aec-462a-8c3b-16c8caddc4f0 read back as promoted

evidence: The operator asked to proceed with all related operator-surface maturity work; the architect created one chapter and tasks 20-29.
A first batch creation attempt failed because the PowerShell helper used for shell quoting was unavailable.
Criteria wording had to avoid commas because task create criteria parsing can split comma-containing text unexpectedly.
Lifecycle export updated a large snapshot even though the architect only needed bounded confirmation that tasks were created.
proposal: Add a first-class chapter commission or task create-batch command that accepts a structured file containing chapter metadata and ordered task specs.
Batch commissioning should admit chapter and tasks atomically or fail with bounded diagnostics, preserving task-number authority.
Criteria should be structured arrays in batch input, not comma-sensitive CLI text.
Return a bounded summary: chapter path, task numbers, task ids, dirty/published posture, and next recommended command, without dumping lifecycle internals.
payload.recommendation: Treat as task-governance and architect-ergonomics execution work.
payload.summary: Commissioning a coherent chapter plus ten related tasks required manual chapter file creation, repeated task create calls, fragile cross-shell quoting, careful avoidance of comma-sensitive criteria wording, and a full lifecycle snapshot export with high-volume output. This is too much friction for a normal architect operation and makes context burn likely.
payload.title: Chapter-level batch commissioning needs first-class CLI support

task.title: Add first-class chapter batch commissioning command
